questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Find the domain of the following functions

\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|}\hline {|r|}{Column - I} & {|r|}{Column - II} \\\hline (A) & f(x)= & (p) & \{n \pi, n \in Z\} \\\hline & \overline{\log [x]} & & \\\hline (B) & \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})=\log \{\mathrm{x}\} & (q) & [2, x) \\\hline (C) & \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})=\sin ^{-1}\left(\log _2 \mathrm{x}\right) & (r) & x \in Z \\\hline (D) & \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})=\sqrt{\log _3((\cos \sin \mathrm{x})} & (s) & \left[\frac{1}{2} \cdot 2\right] \\\hline & & (1) & \mathrm{R}-\mathrm{L} \\\hline\end{array}
questions.solution:
(A) $f(x)=\frac{1}{\log [x]},[x]\gt0$ and $[x] \neq 1$
(As for $|x|=1, \log |x|=0$ )
$\Rightarrow|x| \geq 2 \Rightarrow x \in \mid 2, \infty)$
(B) $f(x)=\log \{x\}$ is defined as $\{x\}\gt0$ which is true for all real number except integers. Hence the domain is $\mathrm{R}-\mathrm{z}$.
(C) $f(x)=\sin ^{\prime}(\log x)$. Domain of $\sin ^{-1} x$ is $[-1,1]$.

Therefore - $1 \leq \log _2 x \lt 1$
$\begin{aligned}
& 2^{-1} \leq x \leq 2 \\
& \frac{1}{2} \leq x \leq 2, x \in\left[\frac{1}{2}, 2\right]
\end{aligned}$
(D) $f(x)=\sqrt{\log _3((\cos \sin x)}$
$\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})$ is defined only if $\log _3(\cos \sin \mathrm{x}) \geq 0, \cos (\sin \mathrm{x}) \geq 1$
$\begin{aligned}
& \cos (\sin x) \geq 1 \text { as }-1 \leq \cos 0 \leq 1 \\
& \sin x=0 \Rightarrow x=n \pi, n \in I
\end{aligned}$

Hence, the domain consists of the multiple of $\pi$, i.e.
$\text { Domain }=\{n \pi, n \in I\}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Suppose that $f: R \rightarrow R$ is a continuous function and satisfies the equation $f(x) f(f(x))=1$ for all $x \in R$. Further, if $f(1000)=999$, then which of the following options are necessarily true?
1. $f(500)=\frac{1}{500}$
2. $f(199)=\frac{1}{199}$
3. $f(2000)=\frac{1}{2000}$
4. $f(235)=\frac{1}{235}$
5. $f(1099)=\frac{1}{1099}$
6. $f(x)=\frac{1}{x} \forall x \in R-\{0,1000\}$
7. No such function exists

Enter the product of the number of all correct options. For example, if correct options are 2 and 3 , then enter 6 .
questions.solution:
For all $y$ in the range of $f$, we have $y f(y)=1$, so $f(y)=\frac{1}{y}$. Since 999 is in the range of $f$, we have $f(999)=\frac{1}{999}$, so $\frac{1}{999}$ is in the range of $f$, then the range of $f$ contains $\left[\frac{1}{999}, 999\right]$ by the intermediate value theorem, so for all $y$ in $\left[\frac{1}{999}, 999\right], f(y)=\frac{1}{y}$. Hence statements 1, 2, 4 are all true.
On the other hand, let $y=g(x)$ be the equation of the line through the points $\left(999, \frac{1}{999}\right)$ and $(1000,999)$ and considerd the function.
$$
f(x)= \begin{cases}999, & \text { if } x \leq \frac{1}{999} \\ \frac{1}{x}, & \text { if } \frac{1}{999} \leq x \leq 999 \\ g(x), & \text { if } 999 \leq x \leq 1000 \\ 999, & \text { if } x \geq 1000\end{cases}
$$
Then the range of $f$ equals $\left[\frac{1}{999}, 999\right]$, and $f$ is continuous, so it satisfies the conditions of the problem. This shows that statements 3, 5, 6,7 are not necessarily true. Hence, the answer is 8 .
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: [Note: $\operatorname{sgn} z$ and $[z]$ denotes signum function and greatest integer less than or equal to $z$ respectively.]
questions.solution: (P) Clearly, number of solutions is 2 . (Q) $$ \begin{aligned} & f(x)=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{x^2}{1+x^2}\right)=\cos ^{-1}\left(1-\frac{1}{1+x^2}\right) \\ & t=1-\frac{1}{1+x^2} \Rightarrow t \in[0,1) \\ & \therefore \quad \cos ^{-1} t \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right]=(a, b] \Rightarrow[a+b]=1 \end{aligned} $$ (R) $\begin{aligned} & 12\left(\tan ^2\left(\tan ^{-1} \alpha\right)+\tan ^2\left(\tan ^{-1} \beta\right)\right)=12\left(\alpha^2+\beta^2\right) \\ = & 12\left((\alpha+\beta)^2-2 \alpha \beta\right) \\ = & 12\left(\frac{9}{4}-2 \cdot 1\right)=3\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { (S) } \sin \left(\cos ^{-1} x\right)=\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\sin ^{-1} x\right)=\cos \left(\sin ^{-1} x\right)=t \\ & f(x)=\sin \left(\cos ^{-1} t+\sin ^{-1} t\right)=\sin \frac{\pi}{2}=1 \\ & \sum_{x=1}^4 f\left(\frac{3 x}{16}\right)=1+1+1+1=4\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
$f: R \rightarrow R$ is defined as $f(x)=\left[\begin{array}{ll}x^2+2 m x-1 & \text { for } x \leq 0 \\ m x-1 & \text { for } x\gt0\end{array}\right.$.
If $f(x)$ is one-one then $m$ must lies in the interval
questions.solution:
for $f^2$ to be one-one $f^{\prime}(x)\gt0$ and $f^{\prime}(x) \lt 0$ for all $x$
clearly $f$ is continuous at $x=0$ and $f(0)=-1$
$x \leq 0, f^{\prime}(x)=2(x+m) \text { for } x \lt 0$
$f^{\prime}(x)$ can not be $\gt0$ for $\forall x \lt 0$ if $m\gt0$
$\therefore \mathrm{f}^{\prime}(\mathrm{x}) \lt 0 \text { for } \forall \mathrm{m} \leq 0$
but $\mathrm{m} \neq 0$ as for $\mathrm{x}\gt0, f$ is constant and $\forall \mathrm{m} \lt 0, \mathrm{f}^{\prime}(\mathrm{x}) \lt 0, \forall \mathrm{x}\gt0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
